bee_quiz.question: The two big groups scientists use to sort all living things on Earth are called what?
The process plants use to turn sunlight, air, and water into their own food is called what?
A mushroom growing in a cave is best classified as a member of which group?
The body part every animal in the Animal Kingdom has, no matter how strange the animal looks, is a what?
The original source of the energy used by every living thing on Earth is what?
The one substance that every living thing on Earth must have to stay alive is what?
Most of a plant's body is actually built from which two things?
bee_quiz.misconception_targeted: Plants eat soil — kids think a plant's body is built from the dirt it grows in, not from air and water.

bee_quiz.question: A coral on a reef is best classified as a member of which kingdom?
bee_quiz.misconception_targeted: Coral looks plant-like or rock-like, so kids assume it isn't an animal — but coral has a mouth and eats other living things.

bee_quiz.question: A bag at the store labeled 'Plant Food' is more accurately called what?
bee_quiz.misconception_targeted: Kids assume a bag labeled 'Plant Food' is the plant's food — but it's minerals (a fertilizer) with no energy in it. The Burn Test proves real plant food is sunlight, air, and water.

bee_quiz.question: In the energy chain Sun → grass → cow → wolf, the wolf occupies which step?
Plants are called the 'producers' of food, so the animals that eat the plants are called the what?
Tracing a pizza back to its energy source, the very last step — the original source — is what?
Which of the following is NOT a member of the Plant Kingdom?
Which of the following is NOT something that every animal has?

choices.c: a way to take in food
choices.d: the ability to eat other living things

bee_quiz.question: Which of these is NOT one of the three things a plant needs to make its own food?

word_cards.definition: One of the very big groups scientists use to sort all the living things in the world.
word_cards.example: Tigers, ants, and people are all on the Animal team. Trees, ferns, and grass are on the Plant team.

word_cards.definition: How a plant makes its own food by mixing sunlight, air, and water inside its leaves.
word_cards.example: A blade of grass uses sunlight to build its body — that's why grass turns yellow if you cover it with a board for a week.

word_cards.definition: A living thing that makes its own food using sunlight. These living things are at the start of every food chain.
word_cards.example: A tomato plant in the garden builds its whole body from sunlight, air, and water.

word_cards.definition: A living thing that has to eat other living things to get its energy.
word_cards.example: A wolf, a deer, a worm, and you — none of you can build food from sunlight, so you all eat to live.

word_cards.definition: An animal that has a backbone — a row of bones running down its back.
word_cards.example: A dog, a fish, a bird, and you all have a spine inside.

word_cards.definition: An animal that does NOT have a backbone inside its body.
word_cards.example: A worm, a jellyfish, an octopus, and a butterfly all have no spine.

word_cards.definition: An invisible gas in the air that plants pull in through their leaves to build their bodies.
word_cards.example: When you breathe out, you let out this gas — and a leaf right next to your mouth could grab it to grow.

word_cards.definition: A bag of minerals that helps plants grow better, but it is NOT the plant's food and has no energy inside.
word_cards.example: Sprinkling it on soil is like giving a plant a vitamin — helpful, but not lunch.

word_cards.definition: A living thing that is not a plant and not an animal — it gets food by breaking down dead stuff around it.
word_cards.example: A mushroom on a forest floor and moldy bread on the kitchen counter are both in this group.

word_cards.definition: The hidden power that makes things move, grow, glow, or get warm.
word_cards.example: The power in your legs that lets you run came from breakfast — and breakfast's power started in the sun.

_self_check.instrument_or_unit_reason: Chapter has no measurement content (no temperatures, masses, volumes, distances, or instruments mentioned)
